organisation.name: The British Pain Society
organisation.overview: The British Pain Society (BPS) is the UK’s largest multidisciplinary professional organisation dedicated to improving pain care.
organisation.missionFocus: Our members span medicine, health, and research, united by a commitment to advancing care through education, advocacy, and collaboration.

services.approach: We work closely with people living with pain to ensure their voices shape our priorities and impact.

careModel.description: Through strong partnerships, we support innovation, raise standards of care, and champion equitable access to effective pain management across the UK.
outcomes: Equitable access to effective pain management across the UK

specialistPainServices.background: Aligned with our vision to help people live well with pain.

programmes.name: High-Quality Education Promotion
programmes.description: Promotes high-quality education in pain care.

programmes.name: Professional Capacity Building
programmes.description: Builds professional capacity through resources and training.

programmes.name: Public Health Advocacy
programmes.description: Advocates for pain to be recognised as a major public health issue.

serviceDesign.structure: Multidisciplinary professional collaboration spanning medicine, health, and research.
serviceDesign.aim: Advancing care through education, advocacy, collaboration, and lived-experience engagement.

philosophy.corePrinciple: Helping people live well with pain.
goals: Recognise pain as a major public health issue
Improve outcomes for people living with pain
